concerns.text: SC-005 states: 'The validity of the synthetic dataset is measured against the criterion that [deferred] of generated proofs are logically valid...' The use of '[deferred]' as the metric value makes this success criterion unmeasurable. A success criterion must define a specific threshold (e.g., '≥ 99%') or a reproducible procedure to determine pass/fail. Without a defined threshold, the system cannot be verified as meeting the criterion.

SC-004 states: 'The statistical power... is measured against the requirement to detect a medium effect size... noting that sample size... is [deferred]'. While the effect size is defined, the sample size (N) is the variable required to calculate power. If N is deferred, the power calculation cannot be performed or verified. The criterion must specify the minimum N required to achieve the stated power, or the procedure to determine N, to be verifiable.

FR-002 requires 'exactly 50 generations' and 'identical total count of rule evaluations'. While '50 generations' is verifiable, 'identical total count' relies on a runtime calculation that may drift due to floating-point errors (as noted in Edge Cases). The FR should explicitly state the verification method (e.g., 'verified by a post-run checksum of rule-evaluation counters') to ensure the requirement is deterministically checkable.
